message:
fix: clean up 65 remaining stale wa/wezterm_automata references

Fixes user-visible GitHub URLs in error codes and error messages,
diagnostic check names (wa-core→frankenterm-core, .wa→.ft),
doc comments, bench fixtures, test strings, e2e harness config,
and remote setup spec. Also creates session persistence epic beads.

Closes wa-2j85j.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

1. cargo check (default) — PASS
2. cargo check --features frankenterm — PASS (all 30 crates compile)
3. cargo test -p wa-core — 2815+ tests, 0 failures
4. cargo test -p wa-core --features frankenterm — 2957+ tests, 0 failures
5. cargo clippy -p wa-core --features frankenterm — 0 errors in wa-core
6. cargo clippy --all — 0 errors across entire workspace

Build time: ~36s clean, ~7s incremental. The frankenterm feature adds mlua/LuaJIT compilation which accounts for most of the build time increase.

Adapter correctness depends on stable fixture inputs and expected outputs for pane/event/triage/history data shapes.

Includes assert_field! macro for field-level validation with actionable diagnostics, variant parameterized tests for all style enums, undo state matrix, and deterministic output verification. 76 total view_adapters tests passing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2i6m","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:13.900466815Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2i6m","depends_on_id":"wa-33fn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:54.967794156Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2imn","title":"Intelligent Snapshot Scheduling","description":"## Goal
Replace periodic snapshot scheduling with intelligent event-driven snapshot triggers that save state at the most valuable moments — when sessions change importance, when risk increases, and when agents complete significant work.

## Background & Motivation
wa-29k1 (SnapshotEngine) takes periodic snapshots (default every 5 minutes). But periodic snapshots are wasteful and insufficient:
- Wasteful: saving when nothing changed
- Insufficient: 5 minutes of work lost if crash happens 4:59 after last snapshot
- Blind: doesn't know whether the work saved was important

Intelligent scheduling triggers snapshots based on events that signal "the state just became more valuable" or "the risk of losing it just increased."

## Technical Design

### Snapshot Triggers
```rust
pub enum SnapshotTrigger {
    /// Agent completed a significant task (tool_use detection, commit, test pass)
    WorkCompleted { pane_id: PaneId, significance: f64 },
    /// Hazard rate crossed threshold (from survival model)
    HazardThreshold { hazard_rate: f64, threshold: f64 },
    /// Agent state transition (idle→active means new session, active→idle means work saved)
    StateTransition { pane_id: PaneId, from: AgentState, to: AgentState },
    /// Extended idle period (good time to snapshot before potential restart)
    IdleWindow { idle_duration: Duration, active_panes: usize },
    /// Memory pressure approaching (save before potential OOM)
    MemoryPressure { rss_fraction: f64 },
    /// Periodic fallback (reduced frequency: every 30 min instead of 5 min)
    PeriodicFallback,
}
```

### Value-Weighted Scheduling
Each trigger has a value score. Snapshot when cumulative value since last snapshot exceeds threshold:
  should_snapshot = Σ(trigger_values since last_snapshot) > snapshot_threshold

This prevents over-snapshotting (many small triggers) while ensuring big triggers (hazard spike) always trigger immediately.

### Integration with Existing Systems
- Survival model (wa-1qz1.1): hazard threshold triggers
- BOCPD (wa-1qz1.2): state transition triggers
- Pattern detection (patterns.rs): work completion triggers (tool_use, commit, test events)
- Differential snapshots (wa-3kxe.3): enables fast event-driven saves

### Implementation Location
- Modify: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/engine.rs (replace periodic loop with event-driven)
- Integration: event bus subscription for triggers

## Existing Code References
- wa-29k1 bead: SnapshotEngine (current periodic scheduling)
- wa-3kxe.3 bead: Differential snapshots (enables fast saves)
- events.rs: Event bus (trigger source)

## Configuration
```toml
[snapshots.scheduling]
mode = "intelligent"          # "intelligent" | "periodic" (fallback)
snapshot_threshold = 5.0      # Cumulative value threshold
hazard_trigger_value = 10.0   # Hazard crossing = immediate snapshot
work_completed_value = 2.0    # Agent completed task
state_transition_value = 1.0  # Agent state change
idle_window_value = 3.0       # Extended idle = good time to save
periodic_fallback_minutes = 30 # Fallback if no triggers
```

## Dependencies
- wa-29k1 (SnapshotEngine): base snapshot infrastructure
- wa-1qz1.1 (survival model): hazard threshold triggers
- wa-3kxe.3 (differential snapshots): enables fast event-driven saves

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event-driven snapshot triggers from multiple sources
- Value-weighted accumulation prevents over-snapshotting
- Hazard threshold triggers immediate snapshot
- Fallback periodic scheduling at reduced frequency
- Integration with survival model and BOCPD
- Unit tests: synthetic event sequences, verify trigger timing
- Integration test: simulate hazard spike, verify immediate snapshot","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:43:46.159935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:28:04.069786Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2imn","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.2","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-09T18:50:47.285161-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-2imn","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:28.386936Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-2imn","depends_on_id":"wa-29k1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:49.365637Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-2imn","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:50.138374Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-2imn","depends_on_id":"wa-3kxe.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:50.806745Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2j85j","title":"Fix 65 remaining stale wa/wezterm_automata references","description":"The rename from wezterm_automata/wa to frankenterm/ft missed ~65 references across source code, docs, tests, and scripts.

## Critical (affects runtime):
- error_codes.rs: 4 GitHub URLs still pointing to wezterm_automata
- error.rs: 2 install URLs still referencing wezterm_automata.git wa
- storage.rs:74-79: SQL table still named wa_meta with columns min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa
- main.rs: 8 references to "wa-core loaded" and ".wa directory" in diagnostics
- config.rs: 3 doc comments referencing .wa directory
- cli_contract_tests.rs: comment referencing .wa dir

## Non-critical (docs/comments):
- lib.rs, error.rs, webhook.rs, tui/query.rs, storage_targets.rs, runtime.rs: ~20 wa-core in doc comments
- Bench fixtures: watcher_loop.rs, pattern_detection.rs, daemon_integration.rs have wa-core strings
- Test files: wezterm_fixtures.rs, typed_client_integration.rs, explainability_e2e.rs
- PLAN.md, PLAN_CODEX.md, RESEARCH_FINDINGS.md, AGENT_FRIENDLINESS_REPORT.md, UPGRADE_LOG.md: old project name
- scripts/e2e_test.sh: header references wezterm_automata
- docs/e2e-harness-spec.md: WA_DATA_DIR env var
- fixtures/e2e/config_baseline.toml: WA_DATA_DIR comment

## Approach
- Source code: targeted sed + manual review + cargo test
- SQL table rename: migration in storage.rs (wa_meta → ft_meta, bump schema version)
- Docs: bulk sed, acceptable to have old names in historical planning docs (PLAN.md etc.)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:49.989868Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:49.989868Z","labels":["cleanup","rename"]}
 {"id":"wa-2ji","title":"Stress test: scalability with 50+ panes and large transcripts","description":"# Task: Scalability Stress Tests

## Goal
Validate wa performance and stability under high load conditions.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Many Panes (50+)
```bash
# Setup: Create 50 panes with concurrent activity
for i in {1..50}; do
    wezterm cli spawn --new-window -- bash -c "while true; do echo line $i; sleep 0.1; done" &
done

# Run watcher and measure:
wa watch &
sleep 60

# Metrics to collect:
# - CPU usage (should stay < 50%)
# - Memory usage (should stay < 500MB)
# - Ingest lag (should stay < 100ms)
# - Queue depth (should not grow unbounded)
```

### 2. Large Transcript (1M+ lines)
```bash
# Generate large output in single pane
for i in {1..1000000}; do
    echo "Log line $i with some content to match patterns against"
done

# Verify:
# - FTS indexing completes
# - Search remains responsive (< 500ms for broad query)
# - Memory usage bounded
# - No disk space explosion
```

### 3. High Event Rate
```bash
# Trigger many pattern matches rapidly
for i in {1..1000}; do
    echo "ERROR: test error $i"
    sleep 0.01
done

# Verify:
# - Event deduplication works
# - No event queue overflow
# - Notifications throttled appropriately
```

### 4. Long-Running Stability (24h)
```bash
# Run watcher for extended period with normal activity
wa watch &
# ... normal usage for 24 hours ...

# Check for:
# - Memory leaks (memory growth over time)
# - File handle leaks
# - Database size growth rate
# - No crashes or hangs
```

## Metrics Collection
```rust
struct StressTestMetrics {
    cpu_usage_percent: Vec<f32>,
    memory_mb: Vec<f32>,
    ingest_lag_ms: Vec<f32>,
    queue_depth: Vec<usize>,
    events_per_second: f32,
    fts_query_p99_ms: f32,
}
```

## Pass Criteria
| Scenario | Metric | Budget |
|----------|--------|--------|
| 50 panes | CPU idle | < 50% |
| 50 panes | Memory | < 500MB |
| 50 panes | Ingest lag | < 100ms |
| 1M lines | FTS query | < 500ms |
| 1M lines | Memory | < 1GB |
| 24h run | Memory growth | < 10% |
| 24h run | Crashes | 0 |

## Testing
- CI integration: run scaled-down version (10 panes, 100k lines)
- Manual: full stress test before releases
- Artifacts: metrics graphs, memory profiles

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test suite exists and is runnable
- Budget thresholds are enforced
- Regressions are visible in CI
- Long-running test detects leaks

 {"id":"wa-2jrdm","title":"Phase 3b: Rewrite git deps to path deps and add external deps","description":"Replace 4 git deps (codec, config, mux, wezterm-term pointing to github.com/wez/wezterm) with 30 path deps pointing to frankenterm/. Add ~80 external dependencies needed by FrankenTerm crates sourced from the upstream workspace manifest.

## Version conflict resolutions
- thiserror: Kept 2.0.18 (ours). wezterm-escape-parser already used 2.0. Upstream workspace had 1.0 but thiserror 2.0 is source-compatible for most uses.
- bitflags: Set workspace to 1.3 (upstream default). termwiz, wezterm-escape-parser, wezterm-surface use bitflags = "2.0" as direct (non-workspace) deps.
- notify: Changed from "8" (ours) to "5.0.0" (upstream). No wa crate uses notify directly — only the FrankenTerm config crate uses it.
- criterion: Changed from 0.7 (ours) to 0.5 (upstream). wa-core uses its own direct 0.7 dep, not workspace.
- finl_unicode: Git dep from wez/finl_unicode fork, branch=no_std, with categories+grapheme_clusters features.

## Key new external deps added
mlua, openssl, bitflags, parking_lot, crossbeam, smol, async-io, async-executor, async-task, flume, libssh-rs, ssh2, pest, pest_derive, phf, cassowary, colorgrad, deltae, nix, winapi, ntapi, and ~50 more.

STATUS: COMPLETED 2026-02-10","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:51:25.758751Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:51:58.524606Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:51:49.967568Z","close_reason":"Completed: 4 git deps → 30 path deps, ~80 external deps added","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2jrdm","depends_on_id":"wa-od8xy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:51:58.524559Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x","title":"Epic: Crash-Resilient Session Persistence via FrankenSQLite","description":"Implement automatic session serialization so that frankenterm can survive sudden power loss and resume all sessions without data loss.

## Motivation
Power loss on trj wiped all WezTerm mux sessions. frankenterm already captures terminal output in SQLite (output_segments), but mux topology (tabs, splits, pane positions), terminal state (cursor, attributes), and process metadata (command, cwd, env) are lost on crash.

## Approach
Phase 1: Use existing rusqlite + WAL to add session state tables and periodic checkpointing.
Phase 2: When frankensqlite (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite) reaches Phase 5+ maturity, migrate storage backend for MVCC concurrent writers, RaptorQ self-healing WAL, and time-travel replay.

## Key Requirements
- Zero-loss guarantee: committed state survives immediate power-off
- Sub-second checkpoint interval (WAL + PRAGMA synchronous=FULL for session state)
- Startup auto-detection of prior session state with restore prompt
- Graceful degradation: if restore fails, start fresh with diagnostic bundle","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:01.099305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:01.099305Z","labels":["epic","frankensqlite","session-persistence"]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.1","title":"Add session_state tables to storage schema","description":"Add new tables to storage.rs SCHEMA_SQL for persisting mux session topology and terminal state.

## Tables to add

### mux_sessions
- session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY
- created_at INTEGER (epoch ms)
- last_checkpoint_at INTEGER (epoch ms)
- topology_json TEXT (serialized tab/split tree)
- metadata_json TEXT (window size, title, etc.)

### mux_pane_state
- pane_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
- session_id TEXT REFERENCES mux_sessions
- cwd TEXT
- command TEXT
- env_json TEXT
- terminal_state_json TEXT (cursor pos, attributes, alt-screen, scrollback position)
- scrollback_checkpoint_seq INTEGER REFERENCES output_segments(seq)
- last_checkpoint_at INTEGER (epoch ms)

### session_checkpoints
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
- session_id TEXT REFERENCES mux_sessions
- checkpoint_at INTEGER (epoch ms)
- checkpoint_type TEXT ('periodic', 'event', 'shutdown')
- state_hash TEXT (BLAKE3 of serialized state for integrity)

Bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 21. Add migration path from v20.
Use PRAGMA synchronous = FULL for writes to these tables (accept ~2ms fsync penalty for durability guarantee).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:12.584976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:12.584976Z","labels":["schema","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.1","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:12.584976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.2","title":"Implement mux topology serializer","description":"Serialize the WezTerm mux session topology (tabs, splits, pane positions) into JSON for storage.

## What to capture
- Tab count and ordering
- Split tree per tab (horizontal/vertical splits, proportions)
- Pane-to-tab mapping
- Pane sizes (rows, cols)
- Active tab and active pane

## Integration points
- Read from WezTerm mux via existing wezterm.rs WeztermClient (wezterm cli list --format json)
- Serialize to mux_sessions.topology_json
- Must handle the case where WezTerm adds/removes panes between checkpoints

## Deserialization
- Parse topology JSON back to a reconstruction plan
- On restore: issue wezterm cli split-pane / wezterm cli spawn commands to recreate layout
- Handle partial restore gracefully (if some panes can't be recreated)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:22.578744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:22.578744Z","labels":["mux","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.2","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:22.578744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.3","title":"Implement terminal state snapshot serializer","description":"Capture and serialize per-pane terminal state for crash-resilient restoration.

## State to capture per pane
- Current working directory (from wezterm cli list cwd field)
- Running command (best-effort: from pane title or process table)
- Environment variables (from /proc/<pid>/environ on Linux, sysctl on macOS)
- Scrollback buffer position (link to output_segments seq number)
- Alt-screen mode (from existing screen_state.rs ScreenStateTracker)
- Cursor position (row, col) — may need escape sequence queries or wezterm CLI

## Serialization format
- Serialize to mux_pane_state.terminal_state_json
- Include a schema version field for forward compatibility
- Use serde with deny_unknown_fields for strict deserialization

## Checkpoint linkage
- Each pane snapshot links to a specific output_segments sequence number
- On restore, replay output from that sequence forward to rebuild scrollback","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:31.758245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:31.758245Z","labels":["serialization","terminal-state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.3","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:31.758245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.4","title":"Implement periodic checkpoint engine","description":"Add an automatic checkpoint loop to the frankenterm watcher that periodically serializes session state to the database.

## Design
- Run as a tokio task alongside the existing capture loop
- Default interval: 500ms (configurable via ft.toml [session.checkpoint_interval_ms])
- Use PRAGMA synchronous = FULL for checkpoint writes only (session state tables)
- Keep PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL for output_segments (high-throughput, acceptable loss window)

## Checkpoint types
- periodic: Regular interval snapshots
- event: Triggered by topology changes (pane created/destroyed, split/merge)
- shutdown: Clean shutdown snapshot with process state

## Deduplication
- Hash the serialized state; skip write if identical to last checkpoint
- Keep last N checkpoints for rollback (default: 5, configurable)

## Config additions to ft.toml
[session]
checkpoint_enabled = true
checkpoint_interval_ms = 500
checkpoint_retention = 5
synchronous_mode = "full"  # for session tables only

## Dependencies
- Depends on wa-2l27x.1 (tables), wa-2l27x.2 (topology), wa-2l27x.3 (terminal state)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:42.740558Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.146659Z","labels":["checkpoint","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:42.740558Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:29.95721Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.052473Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.4","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.146621Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.5","title":"Implement session restore on startup","description":"On ft watch startup, detect prior session state and offer/perform automatic restoration.

## Startup flow
1. Check mux_sessions table for unclosed sessions (no clean shutdown marker)
2. If found, display restore prompt (or auto-restore if configured)
3. Restore sequence:
   a. Recreate tab/split topology via wezterm cli commands
   b. Map old pane IDs to new pane IDs
   c. Restore working directories (cd commands)
   d. Replay scrollback from output_segments (inject via wezterm cli send-text or direct mux write)
   e. Update pane_id mappings in all ft tables
4. Mark restored session in session_checkpoints
5. Resume normal watcher operation

## Config
[session]
auto_restore = false  # true = skip prompt, always restore
restore_scrollback = true  # replay terminal output
restore_commands = false  # re-run commands (dangerous, opt-in)

## Error handling
- Partial restore: log which panes couldn't be recreated, continue with the rest
- Corrupt checkpoint: fall back to previous checkpoint, then fresh start
- Produce a diagnostic bundle if restore fails completely

## Dependencies
- Depends on wa-2l27x.4 (checkpoint engine must have created checkpoints)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:53.314385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.236437Z","labels":["restore","startup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.5","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:19:53.314385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.5","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.236403Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.6","title":"Add ft session CLI subcommands","description":"Add CLI subcommands for manual session management.

## Commands
ft session list              # List saved session checkpoints
ft session show <id>         # Show session topology and state
ft session restore [id]      # Manually restore a specific checkpoint
ft session save              # Force immediate checkpoint
ft session delete <id>       # Delete a saved session
ft session export <id> <path> # Export session as portable bundle

## Robot mode
All commands support --format json/toon for agent consumption.

## Dependencies
- Depends on wa-2l27x.1 (tables), wa-2l27x.5 (restore logic)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:00.637348Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.407852Z","labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:00.637348Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.32316Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.40782Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.7","title":"Session persistence test suite","description":"Comprehensive tests for the session persistence feature.

## Test categories

### Unit tests
- Schema migration v20→v21 roundtrip
- Topology serialization/deserialization (various split layouts)
- Terminal state snapshot serialization (cursor, alt-screen, scrollback ref)
- Checkpoint deduplication (identical state skips write)
- Checkpoint retention (old checkpoints pruned)

### Integration tests
- Full checkpoint→crash→restore cycle using temp database
- Partial restore (some panes missing)
- Corrupt checkpoint fallback to previous
- Multiple sessions in database
- Schema version mismatch detection

### Property-based tests (proptest)
- Arbitrary topology trees roundtrip through serialize→deserialize
- Arbitrary terminal states roundtrip
- Checkpoint sequence with random crashes at any point

### Crash simulation
- Kill process mid-checkpoint (verify WAL recovery)
- Verify PRAGMA synchronous=FULL guarantees (fsync before commit return)
- Test with power-loss simulator if available

## Dependencies
- Depends on all other children of this epic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:10.409219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.493495Z","labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:10.409219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.7","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.493454Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2l27x.8","title":"FrankenSQLite migration plan","description":"Plan and prepare for eventual migration from rusqlite to frankensqlite as the storage backend.

## Precondition
frankensqlite (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite) must complete Phase 5+ (WAL/B-tree/MVCC wired as default execution backend, not just tested components).

## Migration benefits
- MVCC concurrent writers: 50+ agent panes writing simultaneously without single-writer bottleneck
- RaptorQ self-healing WAL: survives torn writes and bit-flips after power loss
- Native mode time-travel: replay any terminal session at any point in time
- XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption at rest (session state is sensitive)

## Migration tasks (when ready)
1. Add frankensqlite as workspace dependency (it's a 23-crate workspace — evaluate binary size impact)
2. Create storage backend trait to abstract rusqlite vs frankensqlite
3. Implement frankensqlite backend behind feature flag
4. Benchmark: single-writer throughput, concurrent writer throughput, checkpoint latency
5. Migration tool: convert existing rusqlite .db to frankensqlite format
6. Gradual rollout: feature flag default off, test in staging

## Status: DEFERRED until frankensqlite Phase 5+ ships","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:22.326998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:35.12062Z","labels":["deferred","frankensqlite","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:22.326998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:30.585744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2l9kn","title":"Integrate cass (session search) for agent context injection","description":"# Integrate cass for Agent Context Injection

## Skills: /idea-wizard, /extreme-software-optimization

## What cass does
Searches and indexes coding agent session history across 10+ AI tools. Sub-60ms search with Tantivy FTS, BM25 ranking, optional semantic embeddings.

## Current state
Standalone CLI at /Users/jemanuel/projects/coding_agent_session_search.

## Integration plan

### 1. Import cass-core as library
- Use cass search engine directly, not via subprocess
- Share the SQLite/Tantivy index (read-only from FrankenTerm)

### 2. Automatic context injection
When a new agent pane starts work on a task:
- Query cass for related past sessions (by bead ID, file paths, error messages)
- Inject top-3 relevant session excerpts as initial context
- Reduces "cold start" problem where agents re-discover solutions

### 3. On-error search
When FrankenTerm detects an error pattern in pane output:
- Automatically search cass for similar past errors
- If a solution was found before, inject it as a hint
- MCP tool: agent can explicitly search cass

### 4. Swarm learning
- After an agent solves a problem, index the session into cass
- Future agents automatically benefit from collective knowledge
- FrankenTerm orchestrates the index-then-search cycle

### 5. Performance (/extreme-software-optimization)
- Search latency: <100ms for top-10 results
- Index update: background, non-blocking
- Memory: shared index, not per-pane copies

## Tests
- Test context injection: new pane gets relevant history
- Test on-error search: error pattern triggers cass query
- Test search latency under 200-pane load (criterion benchmarks)
- Test index freshness: new session indexed within 60s
- **Test swarm learning cycle**: Agent A solves error → indexed → Agent B hits same error → gets Agent A solution as hint. Verify end-to-end with mock sessions.
- **Test index contention**: 200 panes querying and 10 panes indexing simultaneously, verify no deadlock or degradation

## Acceptance criteria
- cass-core as library dependency
- Automatic context injection on pane start
- On-error pattern search
- MCP tool for explicit search
- Swarm learning: solve→index→search cycle verified end-to-end
- <100ms search latency under concurrent load

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Search latency benchmarks:
  - Query latency: benchmark search for varying index sizes (1K, 10K, 100K sessions). Target <60ms p50, <100ms p99.
  - Concurrent search: benchmark 200 concurrent queries against a 100K-session index. Verify no degradation beyond 2x single-query latency.
  - Index write throughput: benchmark indexing new sessions. Target >100 sessions/sec.
  - Context injection: benchmark the full injection pipeline (search + rank + format top-3). Target <200ms end-to-end.
- **Proptest for search result relevance**: Property-based tests for search quality:
  - Generate random session documents with known keywords, search for those keywords, and verify the matching documents appear in the top-10 results
  - Generate pairs of semantically similar sessions (same error message, different context), verify both are returned when searching for the error
  - Generate sessions with no matching content, verify search returns empty results (no false positives from index corruption)
  - For BM25 ranking: generate documents with varying term frequencies, verify ranking order matches BM25 expectations (higher TF-IDF = higher rank)

## Cross-References
- **wa-283h4.11** (LSH error clustering): LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) is used for clustering similar errors in the alien artifact engineering epic. cass's semantic search and LSH error clustering are related search technologies — both find "similar" items in a corpus. Consider sharing the LSH implementation between cass (for session similarity) and the error clustering system (for error similarity). The embedding vectors used by cass could feed directly into LSH buckets.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:24.731773Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:48:20.30327Z"}
 {"id":"wa-2lp7o","title":"Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure","description":"# Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure

## Background
In asupersync, ALL async effects (spawn, sleep, I/O, trace, checkpoint) are gated through a Cx (capability context) token. Every async function must accept &mut Cx as a parameter. This is fundamentally different from tokio where runtime access is ambient/global.

## What needs to happen
1. Define a project-wide pattern for Cx propagation through the call graph
2. Create a wa-core module (e.g., src/cx.rs or src/context.rs) that wraps and re-exports Cx
3. Establish helper traits/functions that make Cx threading ergonomic
4. Create a RuntimeBuilder wrapper that configures asupersync for FrankenTerm's needs:
   - Worker thread count
   - Poll budget
   - Blocking pool size
   - Presets: current_thread() for tests, multi_thread() for production

## Design considerations
- Cx is &mut (exclusive reference), so it cannot be shared across concurrent tasks without scoping
- Each scope.spawn() gets its own Cx — the runtime manages this
- For existing code that passes Arc<Mutex<T>>, the Cx doesn't replace that — it's orthogonal
- Functions that don't do async effects don't need Cx

## Acceptance criteria
- cx.rs module exists with re-exports and helpers
- RuntimeBuilder configuration works with current_thread() and multi_thread() presets
- A simple integration test can spawn tasks using the new infrastructure
- Documentation comments explain the Cx threading pattern

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Measure Cx propagation overhead through call chains of depth 1, 5, 10, and 20. Target: zero measurable overhead vs direct function calls (Cx is just a reference pass). Add `benches/cx_propagation.rs` using criterion.

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest**: Property tests for capability access correctness — verify that Cx-gated operations are only accessible when a valid Cx is held, and that capability tokens cannot be forged or duplicated. Use `proptest!` macro with arbitrary capability request sequences.

## Concurrency verification
- **Loom model checking**: Use Loom to verify concurrent Cx access safety — specifically that &mut Cx exclusivity is maintained across scope.spawn() boundaries and that no data races are possible when multiple tasks hold their own Cx references simultaneously.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:47:51.253433Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:39:48.872292Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2lp7o","depends_on_id":"wa-hj458","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:48:17.308552Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 ### Command
 ```

+ft setup remote --host <ssh_host>
 ```
 
 ### Flags
 - `--host <ssh_host>`: SSH alias from `~/.ssh/config` or explicit host.
 - `--dry-run`: default; prints plan, does not modify remote.
 - `--apply`: executes the plan (non-destructive). Requires explicit confirmation.
-- `--install-wa`: include installing `wa` on the remote.
-- `--wa-path <path>`: optional local path to `wa` binary for scp.
-- `--wa-version <version|git>`: if not using scp, specify install source.

+- `--ft-version <version|git>`: if not using scp, specify install source.
 - `--yes`: skip interactive prompts (only allowed with `--apply`).
 - `--timeout-secs <n>`: per-command timeout (default 30s).
 - `--verbose`: emit step-by-step logs with timings and remote command outputs.
